title: Duplicate declaration of block: 'unnamedblk1'
body:
Verilator fails when there are `begin end` block with a variable declaration and a `foreach` in one scope.
```systemverilog
module Foo;
   function void func();
      int a[2];

      begin
         int t;
      end

      foreach (a[i]) begin
      end
   endfunction
endmodule
```
Here's output of `verilator --lint-only ../bad.v --debug --debugi 5`. I've marked with !!!! the place where "unnamedblk1" name assignment happens.
```
- V3LinkDot.cpp:207:  LinkDotState:

- V3LinkDot.cpp:1057:    TASK 0x154005100 {c2as}  func
- V3LinkDot.cpp:1023:    BEGIN 0x154005a80 {c5ah} [UNNAMED]
```

Do we have here two instances of a Visitor, and each instance starts to numerate blocks from 1?
```
./bin/verilator_bin --version                              
Verilator 5.005 devel rev v5.004-65-g3fe81a383 (mod)
```

OS: macOS Ventura 13.1
